whatItMeasures: Vitamin B12 (cobalamin) is a water-soluble vitamin essential for the formation of red blood cells, DNA synthesis, and the maintenance of the nervous system; this test measures the amount circulating in the blood.
causesOfHigh: Liver disease (hepatitis, cirrhosis — the liver stores B12 and releases it when damaged)
Solid tumours (some cancers release B12-binding proteins)
causesOfLow: Vegan or strict vegetarian diet (B12 is found almost exclusively in animal products)
Pernicious anaemia (autoimmune condition destroying intrinsic factor needed for B12 absorption — most common cause in older adults)
Gastric surgery or atrophic gastritis (reduced intrinsic factor production)
Metformin use (long-term use impairs B12 absorption)
Older age (reduced stomach acid impairs absorption)
causesOfLowSource: NHS. Vitamin B12 or folate deficiency anaemia. https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/vitamin-b12-or-folate-deficiency-anaemia/

high: High B12 from supplementation typically causes no symptoms
Very high levels from liver or blood disorders may be associated with symptoms of those conditions
low: Numbness or tingling in hands and feet (peripheral neuropathy)
Megaloblastic anaemia: shortness of breath, headache, rapid heartbeat

symptomsSource: NHS. Vitamin B12 or folate deficiency anaemia — symptoms. https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/vitamin-b12-or-folate-deficiency-anaemia/symptoms/

citations.title: Vitamin B12 or folate deficiency anaemia
